list.name: Default Changelist
list.comment: The first API element is in place. The route /api/schools/ now returns a list of schools in JSON format, and the site is able to consume that.

MESSAGE.value:
First real push. Models and schema defined.
I haven't quite figured out the migration package I am looking at, so migrations are manual for the moment.

Successfully added a user to the database and retrieved it.
cmd/firstUser successfully added an admin user to the database. I was able to retrieve the user from the database and authenticate the password against the password hash on the user.

Fixed the typos... Oh so many typos. After my extended coding session the other day, at a location without access to a compiler, the resulting code was full of typos and mistakes that prevented compilation. I'm pretty sure most of those are fixed now and I can start moving on to more coding and fixing actual bugs that are bound to start popping up now.

Converted to go module GOPATH development has been deprecated and will no longer work as of go 1.16. So, I have changed the project to a module. Not only does it still work at least as well as it did before, but my shell no longer complains that it can't find anything because of a missing GOPATH.

Adding JSON functionality I have started adding JSON functionality where appropriate. At this time I am able to marshal the important types to JSON so that they can eventually be returned by an API. I created a set of "setup" functions to add data to the database for playing around with as well. This will allow me to start the next step, which will be starting to add error checking that is useful to a web app. After all, we don't want the entire thing to crash if someone attempts to add a type twice, or asks for something that is not there.
The first API element is in place. The route /api/schools/ now returns a list of schools in JSON format, and the site is able to consume that.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: The first API element is in place. The route /api/schools/ now returns a list of schools in JSON format, and the site is able to consume that.
